body.title: Boot-singleton TWM observations (MACHINES_JSON, STATE_INVENTORY, BOOT_SEQUENCE) stack across restarts
body.result: New cortex.twm_evict_source(source) method added. Evict-before-push at three sites: machines_watcher in push_sources.py, boot_sequence + boot_state_inventory inline in main.py. 5 new tests (behavioral + regression guard). 3944 pass.
body.description:
Boot-event sources push single-instance observations at salience=1.0: machines_watcher deposits MACHINES_JSON|initial_load, boot_state_inventory deposits STATE_INVENTORY, boot_sequence deposits the boot-guidance banner. These are semantic singletons — one per instance — but the current push_source pattern appends on every boot. Observed 2026-04-24: two MACHINES_JSON rows (10:09, 10:45) from two boots within an hour, each at salience=1.0. Two STATE_INVENTORY rows ditto. These floor TWM with near-max salience content that can never be displaced by new cognitive observations. Fix: boot-singleton sources should check for an existing integrated=0 row with the same source+category and UPDATE rather than INSERT (or delete prior and insert fresh, whichever is simpler in the push pipeline).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources/machines_watcher_source.py, wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources/boot_state_inventory_source.py, wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources/boot_sequence_source.py (or wherever these three deposit), possibly wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (if a new twm_push_or_replace helper is the cleanest shape), tests/test_boot_singleton_replace.py (new). **Design rules:** oop-first (likely adds a helper method on the source base class), test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — replace-on-push for boot-singleton sources (explicit list: machines, boot_state_inventory, boot_sequence). OUT — changing salience values, extending this pattern to non-boot sources (that's a different design decision per source). **Test plan:** unit test: push MACHINES_JSON twice → only one row exists, content is the second payload. Push MACHINES_JSON, integrate=1, push again → new row present (integrated rows don't block replace). Push non-singleton source twice → both rows present (regression guard).
